2606.26095.title: Learning Action Priors for Cross-embodiment Robot Manipulation
2606.26095.paper_summary: Most Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models build on a Vision-Language Model (VLM) backbone by attaching an action module and optimizing the full policy jointly. This design inherits strong visual and linguistic priors from the VLM, but leaves the action module to learn physical motion almost from scratch. As a result, the policy lacks an explicit motion prior, forcing early optimization to simultaneously discover temporal action dynamics and cross-modal alignment, a challenge further amplified in cross-embodiment settings. In this work, we propose to pretrain the action module with motion priors before cross-modal VLA alignment. Specifically, we introduce a two-stage training framework that equips the action module with cross-embodiment temporal motion structure before VLA training begins. In Stage~1, a lightweight flow-matching-based encoder-decoder action module efficiently learns temporal motion structure solely from unconditioned action trajectories, without processing visual or language tokens. In Stage~2, this learned prior is transferred to VLA training through decoder reuse and early-stage latent distillation, aligning visual-language features with the action embedding space while still allowing end-to-end policy refinement. In addition, the trained encoder serves as a compact history compressor, summarizing state-action histories into a single temporal context token for history-aware modeling at negligible cost. Extensive experiments across 13 diverse cross-embodiment tasks on both simulated and real-world platforms validate the effectiveness of our approach. Compared with VLA training without action priors, our model achieves faster convergence, higher success rates, and substantially stronger performance on data-scarce real-world tasks. Moreover, scaling up the action data in Stage~1 yields a more generalizable action prior that directly improves downstream VLA performance.
2606.26095.paper_summary_zh: 大多數視覺-語言-行動 (VLA) 模型基於視覺-語言模型 (VLM) 的骨幹，通過附加一個行動模組並共同優化完整策略。這種設計從 VLM 繼承了強大的視覺和語言先驗，但讓行動模組幾乎從零開始學習物理運動。結果，該策略缺乏明確的運動先驗，迫使早期優化同時發現時間行動動態和跨模態對齊，這一挑戰在跨實體設置中進一步加劇。在這項工作中，我們提出在跨模態 VLA 對齊之前，使用運動先驗對行動模組進行預訓練。具體而言，我們引入了一個兩階段的訓練框架，該框架在 VLA 訓練開始之前為行動模組提供跨實體的時間運動結構。在第一階段，一個輕量級的基於流匹配的編碼器-解碼器行動模組僅從無條件的行動軌跡中高效學習時間運動結構，而不處理視覺或語言標記。在第二階段，這一學習到的先驗通過解碼器重用和早期潛在蒸餾轉移到 VLA 訓練中，將視覺-語言特徵與行動嵌入空間對齊，同時仍然允許端到端的策略精煉。此外，訓練好的編碼器作為一個緊湊的歷史壓縮器，將狀態-行動歷史總結為一個單一的時間上下文標記，以便在幾乎沒有成本的情況下進行歷史感知建模。在 13 個多樣的跨實體任務上進行的大量實驗，無論是在模擬還是真實世界平台上，都驗證了我們方法的有效性。與沒有行動先驗的 VLA 訓練相比，我們的模型實現了更快的收斂、更高的成功率，以及在數據稀缺的真實世界任務上顯著更強的性能。此外，在第一階段擴大行動數據產生了一個更具可泛化性的行動先驗，直接改善了下游 VLA 性能。
2606.26095.author: Dong Jing et.al.
2606.26095.authors: Dong Jing, Tianqi Zhang, Jiaqi Liu, Jinman Zhao, Zelong Sun, Li Erran Li, Zhiwu Lu, Mingyu Ding
